summary: QA accepts M6-MVP-SPEC-GENERATION-001. The current workspace diff adds the requested deterministic MVP Spec agent, persisted POST /api/opportunities/{id}/mvp-spec route, existing MvpSpec upsert behavior, serialized already-generated spec state on opportunity detail, restrained Generate/Regenerate and Copy Markdown panel, focused automated coverage, and README documentation. Review found no blocking acceptance, validation, documentation, or product-scope issue.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: MVP Spec Agent produces deterministic Codex-ready Markdown with required structure.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts:199-296 builds deterministic Markdown from the opportunity detail input and includes Opportunity Context, Page Structure, Form Fields, Data Model, API Routes, Result Behavior, Monetization Entry Points, Risk Notes, 48-Hour Build Checklist, Acceptance Criteria, and Kill Criteria. tests/mvp-spec-agent.test.ts:59-79 asserts the required section and content surface.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Agent supports injected LLM/safe-JSON dependencies or local generation without secrets/provider setup.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts:111-148 uses deterministic fallback when no LLM client is supplied, uses injected safeJsonCompletion when provided, and falls back locally on failed completion. tests/mvp-spec-agent.test.ts:81-149 covers prompt construction, injected safe-JSON success, failed-provider fallback, and absence of leaked sk-secret/raw prompt text. Focused rg found no process.env, provider construction, OpenAICompatibleClient instantiation, or package/env coupling in the new M6 product files.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: POST /api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec loads one persisted opportunity, generates Markdown, upserts MvpSpec, and returns a safe success envelope.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:85-99 parses the id, calls the injectable generation boundary, and returns { data }. lib/opportunities.ts:400-438 loads one opportunity with the detail include, serializes it, calls the generator, upserts by the existing unique opportunityId, and returns serialized persisted timestamps. tests/mvp-spec-api.test.ts:14-34 covers the success envelope, and tests/opportunities.test.ts:224-304 covers findUnique plus upsert create/update data.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: API safely handles invalid/missing ids, agent validation failures, Zod validation, and unexpected errors without leaking internals.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:48-83 maps not-found, MvpSpecGenerationValidationError, ZodError, and unexpected errors to sanitized envelopes. tests/mvp-spec-api.test.ts:36-149 covers missing id, missing opportunity, validation failures, Zod issues without raw prompt material, and unexpected errors without database secret/stack trace text.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Detail UI exposes restrained Generate MVP Spec and Copy Markdown behavior with empty and already-generated rendering.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: components/OpportunityDetail.tsx:15-18 builds the mvp-spec endpoint URL and components/OpportunityDetail.tsx:140-144 renders the panel. components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:24-50 implements Generate/Regenerate loading/error handling, components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:52-69 implements copy from generated Markdown, and components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:71-132 renders empty/generated states. tests/opportunities.test.ts:338-370 covers empty panel rendering and tests/opportunities.test.ts:373-386 covers already-generated Markdown rendering.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Reopening opportunity detail can display an already-generated persisted MVP Spec.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: lib/opportunities.ts:24-37 adds detail-only mvpSpec selection, lib/opportunities.ts:391-397 loads detail rows with that include, and lib/opportunities.ts:607-637 serializes mvpSpec into the plain detail view model. tests/opportunities.test.ts:186-205 covers persisted spec serialization, including opportunityId, markdown, model, and timestamps.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Focused automated tests cover the required QA surfaces.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: tests/mvp-spec-agent.test.ts covers deterministic Markdown structure and injected LLM/safe-JSON fallback; tests/mvp-spec-api.test.ts covers API success and safe error envelopes; tests/opportunities.test.ts covers persisted spec serialization, upsert persistence boundary, and empty/already-generated panel rendering. The remaining browser-level clipboard/generate interaction is noted as a non-blocking residual risk because current project tests use handler/static-render coverage rather than Playwright.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: README documents behavior, local data dependency, validation commands, single-admin assumption, and non-goals.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: README.md:352-365 documents generated Markdown contents, local Opportunity/RadarTask/SearchRun dependency, single local admin assumption, and non-goals. README.md:358-365 lists the focused test command, npm test, npm run build, and git diff --check, matching the review validation.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Forbidden path and non-goal scope are preserved.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: Status/diff inspection found product changes only in README.md, agents/index.ts, agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts, app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/**, components/**, lib/opportunities.ts, and tests/**. Direct diff/status checks over package.json, package-lock.json, prisma, lib/db.ts, lib/env.ts, lib/radar-tasks.ts, lib/scoring.ts, lib/schemas.ts, app/api/scans, app/api/radar-tasks, app/api/opportunities/[id]/route.ts, app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts, app/opportunities/page.tsx, app/dashboard, app/page.tsx, app/layout.tsx, app/globals.css, services, docs, mock-data, skills, .codex, and .agents produced no product diff output. project/messages/outbox files are JustGoal coordination artifacts.

tests_run.result: PASS: working tree on main shows expected M6 product changes plus the implementation handoff; no unexpected tracked product path was present.

tests_run.result: PASS: no forbidden-scope tracked or untracked product paths reported.
PASS: 26 focused tests passed across MVP Spec Agent, MVP Spec API, opportunity view-model/component, persistence/upsert, and existing opportunity status suites.

PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully and listed /api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec in the route table. Windows @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc optional native package warnings were printed but nonfatal.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings for README.md, agents/index.ts, components/OpportunityDetail.tsx, lib/opportunities.ts, and tests/opportunities.test.ts.
PASS: all 13 active task JSON files validated.

decisions: ACCEPT: All M6 task acceptance criteria are materially covered with no requested product-code changes.
Accepted static React rendering tests for empty and already-generated MVP Spec panel states, supplemented by code review of loading/error/copy branches in components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx.
Accepted injected fake DB persistence tests for upsert behavior, supplemented by code review of the actual Prisma upsert path in lib/opportunities.ts.
Accepted the local unauthenticated POST route because the task explicitly scopes this MVP to a local single-admin assumption and README documents that assumption.
Accepted project/messages/outbox changes as JustGoal coordination state despite project/** being listed in product forbidden paths.
Did not modify product code, package files, Prisma schema, docs outside README, or forbidden product paths.
risks: No browser-level Playwright/manual smoke was run for Generate/Regenerate or clipboard behavior; current coverage is API handler tests, static panel rendering, and code review of client branches.
No live database integration test exercises Prisma mvpSpec.upsert against SQLite; the focused fake client test validates the upsert contract and code review verifies the real Prisma call.
The MVP Spec endpoint remains unauthenticated under the local single-admin MVP assumption and must not be treated as public-deploy ready.
Generated Markdown quality depends on persisted Opportunity detail fields; older sparse rows may produce less rich but still structured specs.
Next build continues to print nonfatal local Windows SWC optional-binary warnings before exiting 0.
requested_action: QA accepts M6-MVP-SPEC-GENERATION-001 with no requested changes. Proceed with remaining required role review/integration workflow as applicable.
